PRIVATE FILE IN REPO: EVOLUTION_LOG.md.prevention: EVOLUTION_LOG.md lives in Claude Projects only, never in public repo

STALE COUNT in CONTEXT_SNAPSHOT.md.auto_fix: python -c "import json; m=json.load(open('system_manifest.json')); v=m['version']; t=m['test_count']; c=m['claim_count']; print(f'Etalon: v{v} | {t} tests | {c} claims')" && python scripts/check_stale_docs.py --strict | grep CONTEXT_SNAPSHOT

README_PPA.md [282] is HISTORICAL DATA -.fix_hint: NOT a real issue. ppa/README_PPA.md contains 'Tests 40-282' as historical record of post-filing additions. The '282' is a range endpoint, not a test count. Fix: skip README_PPA.md from 282 check in agent_learn.py scan_file_for_stale().
README_PPA.md [282] is HISTORICAL DATA -.auto_fix: echo 'FALSE POSITIVE — no action needed. 282 in ppa/README_PPA.md is a historical range endpoint (Tests 40-282), not a stale counter.'
README_PPA.md [282] is HISTORICAL DATA -.prevention: Fix agent_learn.py: if path.name == README_PPA.md, remove 282 from old_counts scan. This false positive polluted 51 sessions.

TRAP-GSD-DEVIATION: stub created instead.fix_hint: After ANY GSD task with 'Deviation noted' in output: copy_file_user_to_claude then bash verify IMMEDIATELY. Do not trust PASS if Deviation was noted. Example: agent_pr_creator.py was a 24-line stub.
TRAP-GSD-DEVIATION: stub created instead.auto_fix: git log --oneline -5 && git diff HEAD~1 --stat | grep -E '\+.*[0-9]+ insertion' | awk '{if ($NF < 30) print "⚠ POSSIBLE STUB:", $1}'
TRAP-GSD-DEVIATION: stub created instead.prevention: Read actual file content after every GSD task. GSD Deviation = high stub risk. Never merge without verifying file content directly.

check_stale_docs rules go stale when tes.fix_hint: In the SAME PR as test count change: update check_stale_docs.py required strings for ALL files referencing the old count. Otherwise rules report false PASSes for up to 13 files.
check_stale_docs rules go stale when tes.auto_fix: python scripts/check_stale_docs.py --strict 2>&1 | grep -E 'STALE|FAIL' && python -c "import json; t=json.load(open('system_manifest.json'))['test_count']; print(f'\nUpdate check_stale_docs.py rules to reference {t}')"
check_stale_docs rules go stale when tes.prevention: UPDATE_PROTOCOL.md rule v1.1: test count change -> update check_stale_docs.py required strings in same PR. GSD must always do this.

Adding claims/agents without GSD creates.fix_hint: ALWAYS use GSD for claim/agent/counter additions. Manual edits bypass the rule-update chain, leaving check_stale_docs.py rules pointing to old values.
Adding claims/agents without GSD creates.auto_fix: python scripts/check_stale_docs.py --strict 2>&1 | tail -5 && python scripts/steward_audit.py 2>&1 | tail -3
Adding claims/agents without GSD creates.prevention: TRAP-NO-GSD: never add claims/agents/counters outside GSD workflow. GSD handles the rule propagation chain automatically.
